answer:
Pipedrive supports two primary authentication methods: Personal API Tokens and OAuth 2.0.

For API token integrations, provide the token in the x-api-token header [1].

```curl
curl --request GET \
--url "https://companydomain.pipedrive.com/api/v2/deals" \
--header "x-api-token: your_api_token"
```

OAuth 2.0 is required for apps published in the Pipedrive Marketplace [2][3]. This involves redirecting users to Pipedrive’s authorization server to obtain an authorization code, which is then exchanged for access and refresh tokens [2][3]. Once obtained, the access token must be provided in the Authorization header for all API requests [3]:

```text
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
```

If both methods are present, the API token takes precedence [4].
